title: Disocactus nelsonii
taxonomy: Disocactus nelsonii is classified within the family Cactaceae, which comprises all types of cacti. It is a member of the subfamily Cactoideae and is further classified as part of the tribe Rhipsalideae. The genus Disocactus is often considered to be closely related to the genus Epiphyllum. Disocactus nelsonii is a unique species within the genus, characterized by its distinctive physical features. Through its classification, Disocactus nelsonii is grouped alongside other epiphytic cacti within the plant kingdom.

intro: Disocactus nelsonii, commonly known as Nelson's night-blooming cereus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family (Cactaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, diabetes, and other chronic conditions. Bioactive constituents of Disocactus nelsonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Disocactus nelsonii is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which may be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
